job_description:
OVERVIEW
Join the fast-growing TechOps BI team at TransPerfect as a Business Intelligence Developer, supporting the design, development, and maintenance of modern business intelligence architectures and solutions. You will work with a suite of TechOps technology products, integrating real-time data from Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services as well as leveraging Snowflake cloud data warehouse environments. You will collaborate cross-functionally to deliver insightful analytics and reporting through leading industry tools (such as KNIME and Tableau), and ensure business stakeholders have access to timely and reliable data to drive strategic decision-making.
DESCRIPTION
• Manage and develop ETL processes using Microsoft SQL Server, Snowflake and KNIME to capture current business, technical, and reporting requirements. Maintain data pipelines and workflows to support real-time updates and accurate replication between various databases and reporting environments. Construct robust processes for error reporting and notification through email and other alerts. (25%)
• Create and deploy scalable analytical systems for end users, including interactive dashboards and reports built on Tableau and KNIME. Develop and automate production reports for daily/weekly/monthly statistics, and design flexible, reliable relational database models within SQL Server and Snowflake. (25%)
• Consult with internal and external stakeholders to understand underlying business needs, applying data mining techniques to discover and unify data from multiple sources. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to architect and implement data storage and reporting solutions for operational and financial audits, including creating new database schemas and automating the delivery of key analytics. (10%)
• Work closely with stakeholders to understand and document data requirements, build unified data sources, and develop interactive dashboards in Tableau reflecting key metrics. Administer Tableau Server environments - including user access, permissions, and dashboard publishing and maintenance. Maintain and enhance published dashboards in alignment with evolving business requirements. (10%)
• Identify opportunities to automate and streamline reporting workflows throughout the organization, enhancing data-driven decision making. Write SQL queries for relational and multidimensional databases (SQL Server, Snowflake) to satisfy complex business needs. (10%)
• Collaborate with teams across the company to ensure seamless integration and synchronization of data between systems, using Snowflake KNIME. Provide technical support for reporting tools and generate ad-hoc reports as needed via SQL queries; implement dashboard and application updates quickly to align with business and end-user needs. (10%)
• Support global operations and production teams by developing scheduled and ad hoc reporting to facilitate monthly billing, project tracking, KPI measurement, and other analytics required by both internal and external stakeholders. (10%)
REQUIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS
Minimum Bachelor’s degree or equivalent.
Proficiency in database and visualization tools required, including but not limited to:
Microsoft SQL Server (Query, View, Stored Procedures)
Tableau Desktop and Server
Snowflake
Microsoft Excel
PREFERRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS
• KNIME Analytics Platform & KNIME Server
DESIRED BUSINESS SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE
• Decision-Making – Make decisions with conviction and display confidence in the process. Take ownership and follow through implementation.
• Availability – Ensure availability to provide assistance, coaching and decision making, and consulting when requested or required by team members or clients. Travel is NOT required for this role, though annual industry conferences may provide opportunity for voluntary travel.
• Problem-solving and planning – Play a key role in problem solving activities and make sure to involve the right people in the planning and problem-solving process. There is an opportunity for improvement in every problem.
• Communication and follow up – Effectively communicate with team members, customers and cross departmental personnel to build strong, effective and efficient communication and follow-up.

# Job Title: Business Management Systems Manager
Location: Farnborough, England, United Kingdom
Role Type: Permanent – Full Time
A key aspect will be to engage with content owners to ensure that content is, and continues to align to QinetiQ’s material risks, adequately describes the risk and the mitigation required to ensure that the risk can be managed in a clear and standard manner
**Your responsibilities will include:**
* Maintain and continuously improve the BMS to ensure its clarity and accessibility to optimise adoption and compliance
* Engage with content owners to ensure that their content addresses risk and mitigation strategies required to manage the risk effectively
* Review and approve new content where required; ensuring that it robustly meets the risk alignment, format and quality required and working with the content owner to improve the content as required
* Promote the BMS to ensure that users understand its purpose, benefits and can navigate the platform to find content easily to aide adoption
**Essential experience of the Business Systems Manager****:**
* Strong understanding of Business Management Systems with respect to document management systems which aide and drive the effective delivery of our business
* Ability to influence stakeholders to ensure that all published content is fit for purpose
* Ability to act as an Ambassador of the BMS ensure that employees, at various levels, understand the role and purpose of the BMS and drive its use and adoption
* Strong written and presentation skills focussed on simplifying and effectively communicating requirements required of a process
* The ability to identify problem areas in the business and take remedial action in a flexible, supportive and pro-active manner
* 3 years’ experience in a similar role from a highly regulated industry
**Essential qualifications for the****Business Systems Manager:**
* Degree educated
* Demonstrable background using SharePoint and Document Management Systems
We value difference and we don’t have a fixed idea when it comes to background or education, provided you can show the required level of experience and willingness to learn then we would like to hear from you.
This role is 37 hours per week based at either our Farnborough, Malvern or Boscombe Down facilities. Hybrid working patterns are available with an expectation that you’ll be onsite 3 days per week.
**Malvern**
The Malvern facility is noted for its work in electronic warfare, surveillance and complex system integration, contributing significantly to the UK’s defence capabilities. Developing technologies for military and civilian applications, including sensors, communications and cyber security.
**Farnborough**
At our Farnborough site exciting work takes place at our state-of-the-art facility, with high-energy laser technologies, our 5m pressurised wind tunnel which has a simulation capability that is unique in the UK and our large research and development projects is a real hub of creativity, research and innovation. Join our talented teams of Engineers, IT & Cyber Specialists, Project Managers, Group Functions Teams and many more to provide future defences in the UK.
**Boscombe Down**
MOD Boscombe Down is a unique operational site featuring the longest military runway in the UK. Primarily focusing on providing testing, evaluation and support services for aviation and aerospace systems. Renowned for its extensive flight-testing capabilities the site plays a vital role in the UK’s aviation defence contributing to the development, testing and airworthiness of military aircraft enhancing operational readiness and effectiveness.

**Job Description**
**Overview:**
Reporting to the Campaign Marketing Manager, the main role will be to drive sales of our vast dental product portfolio by coordinating and uploading all promotions onto the relevant systems, campaign analysis and marketing rebate claims.
You will need to have excellent attention to detail and intermediate to advanced Excel skills to ensure all information is up to date and accurate as you will also be producing ROI and success analysis of all our product campaigns, using advanced Excel techniques, sales data and bespoke business systems.
Attention to detail and deadlines is vital, as is a keen mind for data and the ability to handle a variety of tasks to support the team.
This is a fast-paced environment with lots of moving parts within great marketing teams. You will need to have both a creative mind and be able to manage and manipulate data plus be able to multi-task and manage campaigns without compromising on deadlines or accuracy.
**Job Responsibilities:**
The role holder will be expected to be responsible for the following:
**Summary:**
As part of this role, you will be required to:
* Handle the internal processes and systems including uploading data and promotions to the sales system and work directly with our eCommerce team.
* Liaise with the marketing teams to establish which promotions are current and upload to the sales system and work directly with our eCommerce team.
* Use pricing spreadsheets and understand margins at a basic level.
* Have strong excel skills and an aptitude for data manipulation, measurement, and analysis of large data sets.
* Possess excellent attention to detail.
* Prepare marketing reports as required including ROI on campaigns and promotional activity and supplier sales and manipulate sales data to analyse marketing activity and highlight the findings.
* Prepare, raise, and send invoices and stock claims to suppliers.
* Keep the marketing database up to date, ensuring all records are complete and accurate.
* Raising purchase orders.
* Manage the administration of the advertising programme for the team, ensuring no deadlines are missed, keeping our schedule up-to-date and negotiating with suppliers to secure best pricing.
* Undertake day-to-day administration in support of the marketing team (e.g. filing, photocopying, mailing etc.)
* Flawless spelling, grammar, and proofreading skills.
**In addition:**
* Involvement and assistance with any other marketing activities, as and when required.
* Attend training courses, exhibitions and seminars as required.
* Various monthly administrative tasks.
* Be always an enthusiastic team player by always being approachable and helpful to vendors and other departments. Give support to assist team members in other departments, as and when necessary.
* Aid the Events team to keep the marketing area tidy and pack materials for events.
* Attend external events and exhibitions as directed (infrequent).
**Job Skills & Experience Required:**
To succeed in this role, you’ll need the following:
* **Essential:**
* Excellent attention to detail.
* Experience of data interpretation, manipulation and interrogation and Microsoft Excel intermediate to advanced level.
+ Sort, Filter, Basic formulas, margins, pivot tables & v-look ups.
* Strong communication skills.
* Strong multi-tasking skills and ability to juggle multiple deadlines.
+ Organisational and prioritising ability.
+ Excellent written and verbal communication. Fluent in both written and spoken English - examples will be expected at first/second interview.
+ **Desirable:**
+ Creative flair
+ Analytical and commercial skills and acumen.
* **Useful:**
+ QlikView
+ Experience of an ERP (Enterprise resource planning) software system, such as JD Edwards or SAP would be helpful.
**Education & Qualifications:**
* Preferably educated to degree level but CIM or other marketing or business qualifications would be acceptable.
* Would be required to study and pass BDIA Introduction to Dentistry course.
**All positions are also responsible for:**
* Meeting Company standards pertaining to quantity and quality of work performed on an on-going basis.
* Performing all work-related tasks in a manner that follows all Company policies and procedures.
* Adhering to Company policies, procedures, and directives regarding standards of workplace behaviour in completing job duties and assignments
* This position will be working in an office environment, utilising typical office equipment.
* There will be some travel between UK offices.
**Person Specification:**
We believe the type of person best suited to this role will be:
* Excellent attention to detail is essential.
* Experience of data interpretation, manipulation and interrogation and Microsoft Excel intermediate to advanced level is essential.
* Strong communication skills.
* Strong multi-tasking skills and ability to juggle multiple deadlines.
* Good analytical and commercial skills.
* Organisational and prioritising ability.
* Both a creative mind and be able to manage and manipulate data plus be able to multi-task and manage campaigns without compromising on deadlines or accuracy.
* Experience of marketing is desirable, but more important is a technical ability, creative flair and relationship building skills.
**Our benefits include:**
* A competitive salary
* 25 days holiday a year, with ability to buy up to 5 days holiday each year
* Life Insurance
* Cycle to Work scheme
* Subsidised gym membership
* Access to Health & Wellbeing Apps
* Employee discounts
* A hybrid, flexible working culture

# Care Assistant
## Company Description
Brighter days. Fresh challenges. Exciting opportunities. Plenty of ups, some downs, and a few curveballs. At London Care, every day is different. Every day offers you the opportunity to do meaningful and rewarding work that changes people's lives.
We support service users in the following locations across London.
**WORK LOCATION:**
**Raynes park, Clapham, Brixton, Merton, Mitcham, Wimbledon, Putney, Battersea, Wandsworth, Morden.**
**Pay Rate: Mon – Fri - £14.50 Sat - £14.70 Sun - £15.00 per hour**
**Shifts work across seven days a week,**
**DAY - 06:00/7:00 am – 2:00 pm**
**EVENING – 2:00 pm – 10:00/11:00 pm**
Care Assistant: Role & Responsibilities
Our Care Professionals are the extraordinary people who do the everyday things that mean so much to our clients. Supporting them to live safer and supported lives in their own homes, you’ll follow individual care plans and assist with personal care, helping clients to bathe, dress, manage incontinence, use the toilet and supporting them with their medication. You’ll also help with practical tasks like shopping, mealtimes, and housework.
Helping our clients overcome any mobility problems and other physical disabilities, including helping in the use of, and care of aids/adaptations and personal equipment.
Assisting with domestic tasks such as making and changing beds, tidying rooms, light cleaning, laundry, and emptying commodes.
Our Care Professionals help our clients with personal care such as washing, bathing and toileting arrangements.
Help with eating and drinking by preparing meals, snacks, and drinks, assisting with feeding if required.
Assisting clients with preparing and administering medication.
## Job Description
**What else you'll help with:**
* Companionship – being a friendly face & preventing loneliness through engaging conversations, activities, and outings.
* Personal Care – all aspects of personal hygiene, including bathing, dressing, grooming, and toileting, while maintaining the client's dignity and privacy.
* Medication Management – collecting prescriptions, providing reminders, and assisting with the administration of medication as per care plan guidelines.
* Nutrition and Mealtimes – preparing tasty and nutritious meals, considering dietary requirements and preferences, and assisting with feeding when necessary.
* Housekeeping – keeping their home just the way they like it, including light cleaning, laundry, and tidying up living spaces.
* Mobility Assistance – help with getting around safely, including transfers, walking support, and using mobility aids.
* Health Monitoring – observing and reporting changes in the client's physical or mental condition to healthcare professionals and family members.
* Social Inclusion – encouraging and facilitating participation in community activities and maintaining connections with family and friends.
* Emotional Support – providing a listening ear, empathy, and reassurance to clients experiencing anxiety, loneliness, or distress.
* Documentation – maintaining accurate records of care provided, incidents, and any changes in the client's condition.
* Safety and Emergency Response – ensuring a safe living environment, recognising potential hazards, and responding appropriately to emergencies.
* Collaboration – working effectively with other care team members, healthcare professionals, and family members to ensure comprehensive and consistent care.
## Qualifications
**What you'll need (besides a cape and superpowers!)**
Hold onto your hats, because you don't need any social care experience to join our merry band of care crusaders! We're more interested in your heart of gold and your ability to sprinkle kindness wherever you go. You'll need to be as resilient as a rubber band and as eager to learn as a curious kitten. Get ready to level up your skills and knowledge as part of our close-knit (and slightly bonkers) team!
While previous experience isn't necessary, you'll need to show us that you've got compassion oozing from your pores, communication skills that would make a UN diplomat jealous, and problem-solving abilities that would impress our Moher Teresa herself! Oh, and a commitment to delivering top-notch, respectful care that would make your gran proud!
* Have a right to work within the UK (sorry, time travellers from the future need not apply)
## Additional Information
We see extraordinary achievements happen every day thanks to the talent and commitment of our people. We want to transform the care industry by working smarter, using innovative tech and driving forward positive change. As the largest care company in the UK, we have the size and success to offer you a world of career opportunity, choice and security. Join us on our journey and continue yours.
As a Care Assistant, you'll receive:
* Enhanced occupational maternity and adoption pay.
* Enhanced occupational paternity pay entitlement.
* Death in Service Payment
* Pension scheme
* Benefits and Well-being Platform
* 28 days annual leave (pro rata)
* Cycle to work scheme.
* Refer a friend scheme – earn £300 per referral!
* Blue Light Card eligibility – exclusive staff discounts at big brands for you
* Flexible hours, part and full time available
* Local work and paid mileage
* Enhanced pay for weekends and bank holidays
* Paid training – online and face to face
* Self-development – progression opportunities
* Access to our in-house app
* Work for the largest care company in the UK!
* No experience necessary
So, if you're ready to embark on a care adventure that's more exciting than a rollercoaster ride through a chocolate factory, come join our team of everyday heroes! **APPLY TODAY!!**
We're an equal opportunities employer. Thanks to the commitment of our health care assistants, we see extraordinary achievements happen every day. We are transforming the care industry by working smarter, using innovative tech and driving forward positive change. As the largest care company in the UK, we have care assistant vacancies across the country and we offer a world of career opportunity, choice and security.

**Lead a Global Accessories & Jewellery Category**
At Harding+, we partner with leading global cruise lines and premium brands to deliver retail experiences across ships worldwide. Our Buying teams play a critical role in shaping these experiences - curating product ranges, building brand partnerships and driving commercial performance across international markets.
We are seeking an experienced Category Manager to lead our Costume Jewellery & Accessories category from our Avonmouth head office. This is a high-impact leadership role with ownership of a commercially significant global category, working with premium brands such as Pandora, Swarovski, Mulberry and Coach, alongside driving growth through private label development.
**Define Strategy. Drive Performance. Deliver Growth**
You will take ownership of defining and executing category strategy, balancing commercial performance with a creative, trend-led approach. Working closely with the Category Director, you will shape mid-to-long-term plans, drive revenue and margin performance, and identify opportunities for growth across global markets.
This role requires someone who can confidently translate insight into action, operating at both a strategic and hands-on level. You will be responsible for ensuring the category delivers against key commercial targets while continuously evolving the offer to meet customer needs.
**Own Trading and Vendor Strategy**
You will lead the day-to-day trading of the category, analysing performance to optimise product mix, pricing and promotions. A key focus of the role is vendor management, developing strong relationships with global branded partners while also driving innovation through private label product development, which is a core area of growth for the business.
You will take ownership of range building, product development and vendor strategy, ensuring the category is commercially competitive and aligned to customer demand. This will involve negotiating trading terms, aligning brand and private label strategies, and delivering a compelling product assortment across all markets.
**Lead and Develop a High-Performing Buying Team**
You will lead a team of four direct reports, with wider support across the buying and merchandising functions. Strong leadership is essential, particularly in navigating a complex and evolving team environment.
You will be responsible for setting direction, driving performance and developing capability within the team. This role requires someone who is confident, proactive and able to lead from the front, fostering a high-performance culture while managing competing priorities across a global business.
**Collaborate Across a Global Retail Business**
You will work cross-functionally with merchandising, retail operations and cruise line partners to ensure category plans are executed effectively. You will also engage directly with vendors, attend trade shows and visit ships to stay close to customer trends and market developments.
The role requires someone who is commercially minded, collaborative and comfortable influencing stakeholders across different functions and regions.
**About You**
You are an experienced Senior Buyer, Buying Manager or Category Manager with a minimum of 10+ years’ experience within a mid-sized to large fashion retail organisation. You bring strong branded fashion buying experience alongside proven capability in private label product development, which is essential for this role.
You are commercially driven with a track record of delivering category growth and profitability, and you are comfortable managing complex global vendor relationships. You combine creativity with strong commercial judgement, with the ability to interpret trends and translate these into compelling product ranges and customer experiences.
You are a confident leader with experience managing teams and operating within a fast-paced, high-volume retail environment. You are driven, resilient and comfortable taking calculated risks to deliver results.
**What You Will Gain**
You will join a global retail business headquartered in Avonmouth, Bristol. We offer:
• Annual bonus scheme • Company pension • Healthcare provision • 26 days annual leave plus bank holidays, with the option to purchase an additional five days • Exposure to global retail operations across international cruise markets • The opportunity to shape and grow a key category within a fast-moving business
**About Harding+**
Harding+ is the world’s only dedicated cruise retailer, partnering with leading global cruise lines and operating retail shops across ships worldwide.
With a growing international footprint, we continue to shape the future of onboard retail. We are committed to building diverse teams and creating an environment where everyone feels welcome, supported and able to thrive.
**Apply Now**
If you are a commercially driven, creative Buying leader with a passion for product and a track record of delivering category growth, we would love to hear from you.
**Apply now to take ownership of a global category and drive the next phase of growth at Harding+.**

It connects dedicated buyers and sellers in categories such as trading cards, arts & antiques, coins, and collectible toys. We are seeking a Category Manager Collectibles to take end-to-end responsibility for one or more key categories within the vertical. In this role, you will be responsible for the performance and development of your category – shaping strategy, setting priorities, and driving initiatives that directly impact results.
You will partner with seller-facing teams and cross-functional groups to translate opportunities into outcomes. You will operate within a global environment and coordinate with international counterparts on topics beyond your local market. The role combines structured problem-solving with hands-on delivery across the key commercial levers of the business.
**What You Will Accomplish**
* Lead the performance of your categories, taking ownership for business outcomes and steering the key levers that get results
* Define and continuously refine the strategy for your categories based on market trends, customer behavior, and competitive dynamics
* Identify and prioritise the highest-impact initiatives, and drive them through to execution
* Use data to understand performance drivers, uncover opportunities, and advise decisions
* Act as a strategic advisor to key sellers and partners, supporting their growth and strengthening their presence on eBay
* Identify gaps in supply and selection, and onboard relevant sellers and inventory to improve category competitiveness
* Work closely alongside seller-facing teams and cross-functional partners (product, marketing, finance) to drive execution and remove barriers
**What You Will Bring**
* 3+ years of work experience; prior experience in strategy consulting strongly preferred, e-commerce experience is a plus
* Strong problem-solving skills with a structured, analytical approach and a clear bias for action and tangible results
* Proven ability to translate strategy into execution and thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
* Experience working cross-functionally and driving alignment across diverse collaborators
* Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence internal teams and external partners

Job Profile

**About the Team:**
Working hand-in-hand with our Directed Content and 1x1 Call Services groups, this team provides timely channel checks and voice-of-customer interviews that reveal real-time demand signals, competitive dynamics, and purchasing behavior across priority industry verticals. By identifying the right operator, distributor, or end-customer profiles—and engaging them at the optimal moment in the quarter—we help investors surface differentiated, actionable insights.
Channel & Customer Research (CCR) delivers scalable primary research that provides real-time insight into company performance and industry conditions. Through structured interviews with distributors, resellers, suppliers, consultants, and other channel partners, CCR captures the perspectives of professionals directly involved in how products and services move through the market. Leveraging AlphaSense’s AI Interviewer technology, CCR is able to conduct and scale structured expert interviews continuously, expanding coverage while maintaining consistency and analytical rigor. Covering hundreds of companies across major sectors, CCR provides investors and corporate users with channel research that offers a continuous view of demand trends, competitive dynamics, and market conditions as they develop.
**About the Role:**
Associates are the execution engine for our Channel and Customer Research offering. You will:
* Map the expert universe for assigned verticals
* Source and vet high-value channel partners and customers
* Build long-term relationships with key channel sources
* Coordinate and monitor interviews
* Form sector expertise through regular and consistent research to remain up-to-date on industry trends and news
This role blends investigative research, stakeholder management, and rapid-fire project execution—perfect for early-career professionals eager to accelerate their learning curve inside a high-growth, data-driven organization.
**What You'll do**
* Expert Sourcing & Outreach: Drive email, phone, and LinkedIn campaigns to recruit best-fit experts; negotiate honoraria that balance insight value with cost discipline.
* Screening & Onboarding: Conduct vetting calls to confirm role, recency, and perspective; ensure compliance with AlphaSense and client guidelines.
* Landscape Research: Develop and maintain target lists of distributors, resellers, customers, and other market participants aligned to quarterly coverage priorities.
* Interview Guide Creation: Build and maintain interview guides targeted to each type of call, industry sector, and expert profile - ensuring the best and most relevant insights are captured.
* Interview Facilitation: Schedule sessions at optimal times in the sell-side calendar; monitor calls, troubleshoot tech issues, and capture real-time highlights.
* Quality & Compliance: Uphold rigorous data-privacy standards and maintain accurate records in internal CRM and workflow tools.
* Continuous Improvement: Share feedback on talk tracks, sourcing tactics, and emerging themes to refine our playbooks and coverage roadmap.
**Who You Are:**
* Energetic Team Player: You have a proven ability to manage fast turnaround projects, meet deadlines and targets while maintaining a team-first mentality.
* Adaptable: You are a creative problem solver who is comfortable with changes and new challenges. You thrive in ambiguity and remain resilient when faced with adversity.
* Inquisitive: You are naturally interested in learning and quickly see patterns that help you to identify the best possible experts for a given research project.
* Organized: You have demonstrated an ability to handle and prioritize multiple tasks and deadlines simultaneously and autonomously
* Detail Oriented: You have a strong attention to detail, with the ability to discern and precisely document the core elements of an expert’s relevant skills or experience vis-a-vis a defined scope.
* Excellent Communicator: You’ll regularly be engaging with industry experts to learn about their background and convey what it means to participate in our network. You are comfortable with all communication methods, including phone and email.
* Goal Oriented: You are motivated by the idea of identifying and delivering the best-possible expert profiles for each project scope quickly and efficiently

## Job Description
The
### Channel Business Development Manager (BDM)
is responsible for acquiring, developing, managing and growing relationships with Charterhouse’s channel partners. This role focuses on driving revenue through effective partner engagement, enablement, and joint go-to-market activities. * Find own and manage a portfolio of strategic channel partners, acting as the primary commercial point of contact.

* Achieve channel sales targets through effective partner management and business development.
* Support partners with opportunity identification, qualification, and closure.
* Forecast partner revenue and provide accurate reporting to leadership.

* Develop joint business plans with partners, setting clear objectives, KPIs and growth strategies.
* Support the creation and execution of co-branded sales materials and propositions.
* Identify and recruit new partners aligned with Charterhouse’s overall channel strategy.
### Internal Collaboration
* Work closely with Pre-Sales, Marketing, Product and Service Delivery teams to support partner needs.
* Provide partner feedback into product development and commercial strategy discussions.
* Represent the channel within internal planning and strategy sessions.

* Ability to lead and present sales opportunities on behalf of the channel partners

* Demonstrable track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets.
* Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
* Ability to think strategically while executing tactically.
* Strong commercial acumen and ability to articulate value to partners.
* Self-motivated, results-driven, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.
* Willingness to travel for partner meetings, events, and industry conferences.

Join us, and your benefits and perks will include private healthcare, Medicash cashback, life assurance, workplace pension, cycle to work scheme, access to our employee assistance programme, savings discount, 20% EE discount that extends to your family and friends, a birthday gift voucher and day off, plus more.

**JOB SUMMARY:**
The City of Birmingham is seeking a Chief Accountant to oversee daily financial operations and ensure compliance with federal, state, and local laws, as well as GAAP and GASB standards. This role establishes and maintains financial systems, policies, and internal controls; oversees accounting functions including reporting, reconciliations, payables and receivables, and audits; assists with budget development and monitoring; and supervises accounting staff.
**COMPENSATION & BENEFITS:**
The City of Birmingham provides competitive pay and comprehensive benefits packages to include medical and dental insurance, employer-sponsored retirement plan (pension), generous paid holidays, sick and vacation leave, and more. The pay range for this job is listed below: $81,058 - $125,736
**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:**
The following are job-related qualifications that are required for employment consideration for this position:
Option A:
* Bachelor's degree or higher in Accounting from an accredited college or university.
* Experience performing and/or reviewing financial documents (e.g., audited financial statements), revenue, and the budget.
* Experience directly supervising employees performing financial work (e.g., verifying financial data for accuracy) or supervising employees who supervise others.
Option B:
* Bachelor's degree or higher in Accounting from an accredited college or university.
* Experience as a Principal Accountant in the Merit System.
**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:**
The following are job-related qualifications deemed desirable by the City of Birmingham. These qualifications may be considered by a hiring agency when reviewing applications and inviting candidates to participate in subsequent steps in the selection process.
* Certified Public Accountant (CPA).
* Certified Public Finance Officer (CPFO).
**TYPICAL JOB DUTIES:**
* Oversees the Accounts Payable (AP) and/or Accounts Receivable (AR) process by entering requisitions and payments, generating invoices and account statements and reconciling funds using accounting software in accordance with professional standards to maintain finances for a department or jurisdiction.
* Oversees internal and external audits of financial records by acting as the point of communication, distributing audit-related tasks, and implementing changes identified during the audit consistent with Generally Accepted Auditing Standards (GAAS) and auditor transaction testing techniques to maintain compliance with professional standards and regulations.
* Oversees the maintenance of banking records by researching banking and account information to provide recommendations to the department or jurisdiction regarding which banking institutions to utilize.
* Performs the financial closing process at the end of an accounting period (i.e., monthly, quarterly, yearly) by analyzing account balances and performing balance sheet reconciliations to ensure compliance with with federal. state and local guidelines and laws.
* Oversees the maintenance of the general ledger (GL) by reviewing and approving journal entries, auditing the GL for accuracy, creating journal entries and general ledger adjustments, and reconciling the general ledger to other financial records to ensure completeness of information and compliance with federal. state and local guidelines and laws.
* Engages in activities designed to ensure professional development, awareness of developments in the field, and knowledge of relevant practices, rules, laws, policies, and/or guidelines.
* Supervises staff by assigning and distributing work, directing staff in their job duties, monitoring progress of work, providing feedback and/or training.
**PHYSICAL DEMANDS:**
Job is primarily sedentary involving sitting for long periods of time, but may involve occasional walking or standing for brief periods.
**WORK ENVIRONMENT:**
Work is conducted almost exclusively indoors in an office setting. Work involves use of standard office equipment, such as computer, phone, copier, etc.
**EEO STATEMENT:**
The Personnel Board of Jefferson County provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment with the Personnel Board of Jefferson County, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training. Each Merit System member city/agency operates its own equal employment opportunity policies available on the individual city/agency website.
**ACCOMMODATION:**
To request an accommodation in the application or hiring process due to your own ongoing medical condition or disability, please contact our Accommodations Coordinator at accommodationrequest@pbjcal.org or phone at 205-279-3500 (select option 1) Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Central Time. The accommodation process is reserved solely for job seekers with disabilities requesting accessibility assistance or accommodation in the job application process.

job_title: Citi Commercial Bank (CCB), EMEA & UK Consumer, Retail & Healthcare Head
job_description:
For additional information, please review .
**Overview**
Citi Commercial Bank (“CCB”) is part of Citi, a leading global financial services company serving clients in over 180 countries. Citi’s vision is to be the preeminent banking partner for institutions with cross-border needs, a global leader in wealth management and a valued personal bank in our home market of the United States.
CCB is the bank of choice for mid-sized companies that are looking to grow rapidly and expand internationally. With our global network, comprehensive solutions, and industry expertise, we help these businesses succeed across a wide variety of industries and at various stages of their growth. Our clients see us as trusted thought partner who brings simplicity in an increasingly complex world. We develop client-centric solutions for their long-term growth and pave the way for them to reach across borders and achieve their global vision.
The CCB EMEA Consumer, Retail & Healthcare Head will be responsible for driving CCB’s growth strategy in the industry with a focus on increasing our market share and market presence whilst working with partners across Citi to develop innovative industry solutions. A core part of the role will be to develop talent and ensure bankers have the tools and knowledge to deliver the power of Citi for clients. They will also be responsible for the industry financials and management of our risk appetite and controls inherent in the portfolio and to ensure a strong returns discipline.
The Industry Head will need to demonstrate a strong understanding of the industry including emerging business and disruptor models as well as current and new competition. It requires collaboration and close partnership with senior leadership within CCB as well as key partners within Banking across products and other Citi businesses.
This role requires a leader acknowledged by the market, clients and within Citi as an expert on the industry.
The CCB EMEA/UK Consumer, Retail & Healthcare Head will report to the to the CCB Global Industry Head of Consumer & Retail and CCB Global Industry Head of Healthcare from an industry perspective and to the CCB EMEA and CCB UK Cluster Heads from a geographic perspective.
**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**
**Strategy**
* Size EMEA / UK mid-market growth opportunity and develop a strategy to capture it
* Identify emerging trends, including disruptors and new sub-sectors
* Optimise resource allocation aligned with EMEA / UK strategy
* Be visible as an industry leader in the market and internally
* Execution of Citi initiatives across the franchise
**Business Development & Sales Management**
* Drive innovation including development and commercialisation of industry specific solutions in partnership with products / regions / countries and leveraging digital tools and platforms
* Collaborate with product and risk partners to ensure we deliver One Citi to our clients. Own, respond to, uncover and anticipate future needs, roadblocks or risks and expectations
* Drive industry thought leadership externally and internally and drive presence in industry ecosystem
* Co-ordinate industry webinars, client events and external marketing
* Establish and lead robust sales management processes for pipeline management, new client acquisition, client penetration and winning share of wallet
* Responsibility for industry financials including capital returns
**Risk Management**
* Establish risk appetite for sub-sectors, including target market definition and risk framework
* Responsibility for quality of credit portfolio, origination in line with Target Market, prompt problem recognition, and adherence with regulatory requirements and Citi policies
* Lead industry portfolio reviews, understand emerging industry trends and impact to CCB portfolio, work with CLM and Risk to develop risk mitigation strategies
**Talent**
* Recruit, build out and retain best-in-class commercial banking team
* Manage and motivate team, ensuring an aligned organisational model with scorecards and KPIs to monitor business performance
* Talent development and coaching to provide stretch targets and fulfilling assignments
* Build industry expertise and skills within the industry team and ensure deep understanding of relevant products and solutions
* Foster global and cross-country connectivity for sharing of best practices
**Knowledge / Experience**
* 15+ years of commercial/corporate banking experience with demonstrated industry expertise
* In-depth knowledge of all aspects of commercial/corporate banking products including treasury management, trade, markets, lending, capital markets and strategic advisory, with a particular focus on delivering Citi’s global network
* Significant experience in a credit decision making authority, with experience in complex capital structures and cross border transactions
* Deep experience in pitching and executing capital markets and strategic solutions, including joint planning and calling with investment bankers
* Possesses sound judgement and is creative in providing structured solutions to clients
* Business builder capable of creating a vision and motivating the team
* Capability to demonstrate advanced level credit skills to be appointed a senior credit officer
**Skills & Competencies**
* Highly focused, able to rapidly determine key priorities, clearly communicate those priorities and ensure resources are properly aligned
* Strong execution skills, results-driven with sound financial acumen
* Sharp business focus, demonstrating innovative approaches and entrepreneurial mindset
* A team player. Proven ability to collaborate effectively with peers and partners, fostering strong relationships to ensure robust client delivery
* Skilled in managing and leading teams through complex organisational change, and recognised for attracting, developing, and retaining top talent
* A leader who embodies strong client intensity and drives an innovative winning culture and is abreast of digital and AI developments to empower clients and teams
* Proven leadership ability with excellent interpersonal communication skills
* Able to quickly adapt priorities based on the latest data insights
* Excellent client interaction and management skills
* High level of personal integrity
* Ability to work effectively in a matrix environment
**Qualifications**
* Bachelor's degree/University degree or equivalent experience
* Master's/MBA degree preferred
* Senior Credit Officer or Senior Credit Officer candidate.
### Candidates applying for this role must be aware that it is a Certified Role, subject to the FCA and PRA Certification Regime.
### The Certification Regime is one element of the Individual Accountability Regime which came into effect on 7 March 2016.
### Under the Certification Regime, Citi UK regulated entities (Citi entities) must ensure that employees working in certain roles categorised as specified significant harm functions (Certified Roles) are assessed as fit and proper to carry out their role.
### Under the guidance provided by the FCA and PRA, firms should have regard to the following when assessing fitness and propriety:
### • Honesty, integrity and reputation
### • Financial soundness
### • Competence and capability
### ### In order to comply with the requirements of the Certification Regime, Citi entities must take reasonable care to ensure that an employee does not perform a Certified Role without first being certified as Fit and Proper. For this reason, you will be assessed for this role against the Fit and Proper requirements, as described above.
### This assessment will be carried out through extensive interviews, self-disclosures, permitted criminal record checks, regulated reference checks, credit checks and other background screening checks.

### Citrix Lead Account Technology Strategist (ATS) - UK Industry
### **Req ID:** R104490
###
### Citrix Lead Account Technology Strategist (ATS) - UK Industry
* Remote
* Remote, United Kingdom
* London, England, United Kingdom
* Citrix
The Account Technology Strategist (ATS) plays a pivotal role in aligning technologies with customer business objectives and challenges, fostering innovation and broad adoption. The ATS works closely with a specific set of Enterprise customers, understanding their needs and the challenges they need to overcome. The ATS then guides each customer to retain and grow their use of Citrix technologies to achieve their objectives and create business value
This role is mapped to our Enterprise Industry region and will report to the Account Technology Strategist (ATS) leader for that region. Those applying for the role should be based in the United Kingdom.
Travel to the office, customers and events is expected as part of the role and can be as much as 60%
\*Although aligned with the Lead ATS level, we are open to considering exceptional candidates for a higher-grade ATS role when appropriate.
**Duties and Responsibilities**
As a seasoned technology professional with field experience, account technology strategists operate within the Citrix Customer Sucess function; the ATS role: * Understands and leverages industry trends and insights.
* Is accountable for assessing and delivering the potential of Citrix products to meet our customers’ business needs.
* Provides technical expertise for the Citrix Platform throughout the entire customer lifecycle.
* Achieves trusted advisor status and is known as a customer advocate.
* Has strong knowledge of their customers' use cases and understands which Citrix solutions can be used to deliver increased value for these use cases.
* Conducts product demonstrations and technical presentations remotely or at customer locations and Citrix facilities.
* Orchestrates and coordinates cross-functional resources to ensure customer needs are met.
* Has the ability to present and build complex, multi-product proof of concept solutions to gain customer commitment for our technology.
* Assists with overcoming technical and competitive objections and accelerates adoption.
* Manges their professional network of key decision-makers within their assigned accounts and develop extended relationships beyond our current contacts.
* Has the ability to engages at both the technical decision maker and executive level within their customer.
* Take a proactive approach to developing their customers, progressing initiatives and helping deliver customer outcomes
* Serves as an external spokesperson and evangelist for Citrix’s vision and associated technologies.
* Maintains and updates account intelligence within our systems of record.
**Required Experience/Skills**
The successful candidate will be able to demonstrate deep understanding and experience of:
* Citrix's competitive domain and technologies, such as end user compute, desktop as a service, virtual desktop infrastrcutre, application delivery and zero trust network access.
* Managing a variety of customer or partner stakeholders to achieve speciic outcomes.
* Prioritising their workload, context-switching between customers and projects as demands change.
* Diffusing technical or political situations with partners and customers.
* Operating in a fast and dynamic business environment.
The successful candidate must also:
* Be flexible, dependable and capable of quickly learning new products and technologies.
* Exhibit a strong work ethic and a positive attitude; remaining accountable for actions and progress within their initiatives.
* Maintain accurate records and complete required administrative activities in our systems of record (e.g. expenses, CRM updates, feedback questionnaires).
* Possess excellent oral and written communications skills
* Be able to travel meet with customers, partners and attend corporate events & meetings.
As this specific role sits within the Industry team, any knowledge of Telecommunications, Media, Technology or ISV organisations would be beneficial.

# Client System and Product Onboarding Administrator
Vacancy Salary
£26,500

*This is a Temporary, Full Time vacancy that will close in 7 days at 23:59 BST.*
**The Vacancy**
At Evri, our technology underpins everything we do - and getting our clients set up right from day one is critical to their success. Behind every successful client relationship is a seamless onboarding experience, powered by people who care about accuracy, service and delivery.
If you’re organised, detail‐driven and enjoy working at the intersection of technology and client service, this could be the perfect role for you.
Join us as an **IT Client Onboarding Administrator** and play a key part in setting our clients up for success across Evri’s technology platforms.
**What you’ll be doing**
As an IT Client Onboarding Administrator, you’ll be responsible for ensuring new clients - and new services for existing clients - are configured accurately and efficiently across our IT systems.
Using **ServiceNow**, you’ll manage onboarding tasks, incidents and service requests, supporting a smooth end‐to‐end experience while ensuring SLA compliance and high data quality.
**Key responsibilities**
* Set up and configure new clients within Evri IT systems accurately and on time
* Manage onboarding‐related tasks, incidents and service requests via ServiceNow
* Ensure requests are logged, tracked and completed in line with agreed SLAs
* Maintain accurate client records and data integrity at all times
* Monitor and report on onboarding progress against agreed requirements
* Update and maintain process documentation to reflect current practices
* Manage workload effectively, prioritising tasks in a fast‐paced environment
* Build strong working relationships with internal stakeholders across IT and account teams
**What you’ll bring**
We’re looking for someone who is organised, client‐focused and confident working with IT systems and processes.
* Experience in an IT support, onboarding or administrative role
* Strong attention to detail with excellent data entry accuracy
* Clear written and verbal communication skills
* Confidence using **ServiceNow** or similar ITSM tools
* Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines and SLAs
* A logical, methodical approach to work and problem‐solving
* Knowledge of ITIL or IT Service Management principles is desirable
* IT‐related qualifications or certifications (e.g. ITIL, CompTIA) are advantageous but not essential
At Evri, we’re creating a workplace where people feel valued, supported and able to grow. You’ll join a collaborative IT team that’s focused on continuous improvement, knowledge sharing and delivering great outcomes for our clients.
We’re proud of our inclusive culture and flexible approach to working - because we know great people do their best work when they feel supported.
**We are Evri. Where everyone is welcome.**
**We’re excited for the future. Let’s shape it together.**
*Every parcel, every person, every place – every delivery made for you.*
Evri is leading the way in creating responsible delivery experiences for everyone, everywhere. And we’re doing that by offering the most convenient way to send, receive and return parcels without costing the earth.
Whether it’s a customer sending a present to a friend, a marketplace trader shipping in bulk or a major retailer such as John Lewis, asos or Next, we’ve got it covered. With our team of 30,000 couriers, thousands of local ParcelShops and a growing network of hubs and depots across the country, we safely get parcels from A to everywhere.
And it’s not just customers and retailers who trust us. We’ve won loads of industry awards over the past few years, including gongs for IT, innovation and our green fleet.
But we wouldn’t be the biggest dedicated parcel delivery company in the UK without amazing people. We’re a friendly, ambitious and diverse bunch, united by a passion for delivering the best customer experience. job_title: Cloud 12-Month University Placement (2026-27) Technology Sales Intern London, GB
job_description:
Introduction
At IBM Global Sales, we bring together innovation, collaboration, and expertise to help clients solve their most complex business challenges. Working across industries and geographies, you’ll partner with colleagues, clients, and partners to co-create solutions that drive digital transformation and lasting impact.Success in Global Sales is built on curiosity, empathy, and collaboration. You’ll connect technical understanding with strong people skills, building trusted relationships and shaping solutions that improve business and society. With world-class onboarding, continuous learning, and a supportive culture, IBM offers the tools and opportunities to grow your career. Join us and be part of a global team that’s passionate about driving innovation and making a difference.
Your role and responsibilities
**IMPORTANT:** Please apply using the application form via instead of your CV.
This internship sits within IBM’s Technology Sales organisation. You’re not here to develop software—you’re here to sell technology, support sales teams, and help us show clients why IBM is the right choice. Think of this as your backstage pass to the world of tech—not coding it, but selling it, pitching it, demoing it, and showing clients why it actually matters.
Technology Sales is fast paced, people focused, and packed with opportunities to make a real impact early in your career. It’s where you learn how companies choose the tech that runs their business, how big deals develop, and how powerful solutions like AI, Cloud, and Automation actually influence the world around us.
**What you’ll do**
As a Technology Sales Intern at IBM, you’ll:
* work with major enterprise clients
* get exposure to senior leaders
* help build demos and value stories
* contribute to real sales plays
* and see firsthand how IBM technology wins deals
If you want a placement year that’s dynamic, hands on, and genuinely career defining—not just shadowing teams or doing admin—this is it.
**Depending on your skills and interests, you might support:**
* Larger Enterprise Accounts
* Customer Success
* Partner Sales
* Sales enablement, Operations & seller productivity
* Brand Specific Teams e.g. Data & AI and Automation
* or strategic teams in the UKI General Manager’s Office or UKI Chairman’s Office
Every path puts you close to real clients, real deals, and real business impact.
**Why this role matters**
You'll be involved in key actions across the sales organisations, making a direct impact by:
* Qualifying leads and spotting opportunities where IBM tech can solve real business problems
* Helping sellers prepare for client engagements with research, insights, and value focused materials
* Supporting product demos, prepping client presentations, and helping simplify technical concepts
* Assisting customer success teams as they guide clients through onboarding and adoption
* Supporting partner teams with enablement, co selling resources, and go to market activities
* Helping sales operations with dashboards, tools, seller resources, and productivity improvements
* Contributing to sales plays and go to market campaigns across the Cloud, Data & AI portfolio
* Supporting senior leaders on strategic projects, briefings, or executive level client work
* Collaborating across sales, technical specialists, marketing, customer success, partners and more
**What We’re Looking For**
* You love explaining things—especially tech—in a way people can actually understand.
* You’re curious about how organisations buy tech and what influences those decisions.
* You like working with people and collaborating across teams.
* You want to understand Cloud, Data & AI, Security, and Automation—not just academically, but commercially.
* Access online learning tools, training sessions, and development opportunities designed to help you grow.
* Be supported by two managers plus a network of Early Professionals and HR colleagues.
* Make connections across the business with leaders, interns and teams from every area of IBM
**What you’ll bring**
You communicate clearly—tailoring messages to your audience—and you’re organised, collaborative, and accountable. You’re proactive, comfortable troubleshooting, and you use your initiative to move work forward. You sweat the details and back your ideas with data. You have proven proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, and you’re good with data—able to analyse and manipulate it to extract meaningful insights.
Impact, learning, and collaboration
You’ll work with supportive teams, gain hands‐on experience with leading marketing and event tech, and see the tangible impact of your work—right from IBM’s York Road office in London.You'll also join our Early Professional Community - known for being supportive, social, and collaborative. Occasional travel may be required for events or projects.
**Competitive Benefits**
* £25,000 salary
* 25 days holiday + public holidays
* IBM Rewards: discounts at major retailers
Required education
Pre-University Qualification
* Studying at a UK University
* Completing an undergraduate degree and on track for a 2:1 (minimum)
* Due to graduate in 2028 - you must return to university to complete your final year after placement.
* Live within a commutable distance of your base location (London) as you will be required on site.
* Eligible to work in the UK full time for the duration of your internship (no visa sponsorship available)
Preferred technical and professional experience
Bonus Points If You Have
* Exposure to cloud, software, or AI through uni projects or personal interest
* Experience presenting, pitching, or simplifying complex ideas
* An interest in big picture thinking and enterprise level projects
### ABOUT BUSINESS UNIT
IBM has a global presence, operating in more than 175 countries with a broad-based geographic distribution of revenue. The company’s Global Markets organization is a strategic sales business unit that manages IBM’s global footprint, working closely with dedicated country-based operating units to serve clients locally. These country teams have client relationship managers who lead integrated teams of consultants, solution specialists and delivery professionals to enable clients’ growth and innovation. By complementing local expertise with global experience and digital capabilities, IBM builds deep and broad-based client relationships. This local management focus fosters speed in supporting clients, addressing new markets and making investments in emerging opportunities. Additionally, the Global Markets organization serves clients with expertise in their industry as well as through the products and services that IBM and partners supply. IBM is also expanding its reach to new and existing clients through digital marketplaces.
In a world where technology never stands still, we understand that, dedication to our clients success, innovation that matters, and trust and personal responsibility in all our relationships, lives in what we do as IBMers as we strive to be the catalyst that makes the world work better.
Being an IBMer means you’ll be able to learn and develop yourself and your career, you’ll be encouraged to be courageous and experiment everyday, all whilst having continuous trust and support in an environment where everyone can thrive whatever their personal or professional background.
Our IBMers are growth minded, always staying curious, open to feedback and learning new information and skills to constantly transform themselves and our company. They are trusted to provide on-going feedback to help other IBMers grow, as well as collaborate with colleagues keeping in mind a team focused approach to include different perspectives to drive exceptional outcomes for our customers. The courage our IBMers have to make critical decisions everyday is essential to IBM becoming the catalyst for progress, always embracing challenges with resources they have to hand, a can-do attitude and always striving

# Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
**Please note: this role is Remote UK, no overseas candidates or work from overseas will be acceptable due to nature of our customer data management policies. Travel to client sites although very unlikely may occur and will be fully reimbursed by Kerv Group.**
Launched in July 2020, the Kerv Group is a next-generation customer-first, cloud-first provider, created to help customers achieve successful business outcomes in a new digital era. We are undergoing an exciting period of transformation and growth following the acquisition of seven highly successful organisations, and we are now over 900 employees.
**About the Role**
As a result of growth in a new area of business, we are looking for an operationally focused individual to take ownership of our production and development AWS and Azure infrastructure. Main focus by far will be AWS.
We currently host software for our own products, as well as custom software for 30+ customers across five AWS tenants and one Azure tenant. These environments support a range of applications and customers, from SMEs to large public-sector organisations, some of whom require CIS-compliant infrastructure. This role is the first step in creating a more mature infrastructure management capability. This role is a combination of hands-on cloud operations, security remediation, change control, automation and cost optimisation. **Your part to play**
You will help run and improve our AWS and Azure environments across production and development, with a strong focus on security, stability, compliance and cost control. Working with our existing security tooling, including OX Security and Wiz, you will identify and prioritise vulnerabilities, drive remediation activity, and maintain a forward plan to reduce risk and improve the security posture of our customer and internal environments. This includes owning patching and security updates across the estate, ensuring critical and high vulnerabilities are addressed within agreed timescales in line with Cyber Essentials and our customer obligations. You will work within our change control process, making sure infrastructure changes are properly assessed, documented, scheduled and communicated. You will also look for opportunities to automate repeatable operational tasks, improve consistency, reduce manual effort and support ongoing cost optimisation across our cloud estate. As part of a small team, you will work closely with engineering, service and product colleagues to keep our platforms secure, resilient and efficient. You will also support the Service Desk with escalations where needed. You will also: • Manage the day-to-day operation of AWS and Azure environments across multiple customer and internal estates. • Plan and deliver patching, system updates and infrastructure changes. • Use OX Security, Wiz and other tools to identify, triage and track vulnerabilities through to resolution. • Maintain a prioritised programme of work covering remediation, technical debt and broader security improvements. • Contribute technical input into change control, including impact assessments, implementation plans, rollback plans and scheduling. • Maintain and improve CIS-aligned configurations and controls where required. • Drive cost optimisation initiatives across AWS and Azure, including usage reviews, rightsizing and reducing waste. • Improve operational efficiency through automation, scripting and better tooling. • Support incident resolution, root cause analysis, documentation and continuous improvement. **All about You**
You must have: • Hands-on experience supporting cloud infrastructure in live production environments, with strong experience in AWS or Azure and working knowledge of the other. Preferably AWS. • Experience of day-to-day infrastructure operations, including patching, vulnerability remediation and implementing production changes. • Experience working within a controlled change process in a production environment. • A solid understanding of cloud security fundamentals, including identity and access management, networking, platform hardening and general security best practice. • Experience using automation or scripting to reduce manual effort and improve operational consistency. • Strong organisational, troubleshooting and communication skills, with the ability to prioritise work and see actions through to completion. • Awareness of security and compliance requirements such as Cyber Essentials, CIS or similar good practice.
Ideally you will also have: • Experience across both AWS and Azure. Preferably AWS. • Experience with OX Security, Wiz, or similar cloud security and posture management tooling. • Experience supporting multi-customer or multi-tenant environments. • Experience with Infrastructure as Code or configuration and deployment tooling such as Terraform, Ansible, Jenkins, PowerShell, Bash or Python. • Experience supporting public-sector or other regulated environments. • Experience identifying and delivering cloud cost optimisation initiatives. • Experience supporting operational escalations or working closely with a Service Desk. • Relevant certifications such as AWS, Azure, security or ITIL qualifications.

Simpson Associates transforms raw data into actionable insights that drive positive change.
Our Microsoft data expertise, our specialist sector knowledge, plus our innovative and trusted advice and guidance are just some of the reasons clients choose to work with us.
Our mission is to help purpose-led organisations from within the public and private sectors to harness data as a lever for change and enable them to realise business value more quickly. We provide the full range of services to support organisations on their data transformation journey. From advisory support and data strategy, to developing Data & AI solutions, right through to providing a range of managed services.
We are a Microsoft Solutions Partner, holding Specialisations in AI Platform on Microsoft Azure, Analytics on Microsoft Azure, Data Warehouse Migration to Microsoft Azure and Migrate Enterprise Applications to Microsoft Azure, as well as holding Solutions Partner designations in Data & AI (Azure); Digital & App Innovation (Azure); Infrastructure (Azure) and Security.
But it's not just about the badges. We are proud to be recognised as the winner of the 2024 Microsoft Community Response Partner of the Year award, reflecting our dedication to using technology for positive change. We are also a Databricks partner, and an IBM Gold Partner, specialising in Cognos Analytics and Planning Analytics.
With offices in York and Sheffield, and a team based throughout the UK – we champion creativity, innovation and collaboration in the workplace.
**The Role**
As a Cloud Platform Consultant within the Cloud Platform and Security Team, you will be responsible for designing, deploying, and managing cloud infrastructure solutions on the Microsoft Azure platform. Your role will involve architecting cloud environments, implementing infrastructure as code and optimising cloud resources for performance and cost-efficiency. You will also develop a growing awareness of security best practices across cloud workloads, working alongside our security specialists to ensure solutions meet our clients’ security and compliance requirements.
You will provide expert guidance on cloud strategy, assist in cloud migration and modernisation projects, and ensure that our clients’ needs are met with the highest standards of service. You will collaborate closely with our Cloud Platform and Security Team colleagues, contributing to a culture of continuous improvement and knowledge sharing. Excellent stakeholder management skills are essential as you will interact with a broad spectrum of customers and internal team members.
**Key Responsibilities**
* Design and implement Azure cloud infrastructure solutions, ensuring scalability, reliability, and security.
* Manage and optimise Azure resources for cost and performance efficiency.
* Support cloud migration projects, including planning, execution, and post-migration support.
* Implement and manage a range of Azure services such as Azure Virtual Machines, Azure Storage, Azure Databricks, Microsoft Sentinel and more.
* Automate deployment and management processes using Azure Bicep (infrastructure as code) and by utilising tools such as Azure CLI and Azure DevOps.
* Monitor and troubleshoot cloud infrastructure to ensure high availability and performance.
* Ensure compliance with security best practices and policies in the cloud environment, working in partnership with the security specialists within the Cloud Platform and Security Team.
* Provide ongoing support and maintenance for cloud infrastructure.
* Participate in the on-call rota, supporting our managed service customers.
* Develop and maintain internal policies, procedures, standards, baselines, and guidelines where applicable.
**Technical Requirements**
* Experience with Cloud networking, including Virtual Networks and Azure Firewall.
* Experience with Azure DevOps and infrastructure as code, such as Azure Bicep and Terraform.
* Experience in deploying a broad range of Azure resource types, including (not limited to) Storage accounts, Virtual machines, Synapse Databricks.
* Strong scripting skills in PowerShell, Python, or similar.
* Hands-on experience with continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines.
* Working knowledge of Azure services.
* Familiarity with version control systems, especially Git.
* Experience with monitoring tools such as Azure Monitor, Application Insights, or similar.
* Knowledge of security best practices in cloud environments.
* Understanding of network setup and configuration in Azure.
* Working familiarity with Microsoft Sentinel and Microsoft Defender services, and an interest in developing deeper security skills as part of the Cloud Platform and Security Team.
* Familiarity with the Cloud Adoption Framework and the Azure Landing Zone conceptual architecture.
* Experience configuring and administrating Identity and Access Management tooling such as Active Directory and EntraID.
**Skills and Attributes Required**
* A natural problem solver with excellent troubleshooting skills.
* The ability to prioritise workload under pressure and deliver to tight deadlines.
* Strong team player with excellent communication skills.
* Enthusiastic, confident, and proactive.
* Pleasant, friendly, and approachable manner, with empathy for customers.
**Advantageous Qualifications and Skills**
* Microsoft 365 and related services.
* Microsoft Defender suite.
* Microsoft Intune and Microsoft Sentinel.
* Atlassian Jira.
* Microsoft certifications AZ-104, AZ-400, AZ-305, AZ-500 and SC-200.
* Presenting Skills and Confidence speaking in front of an audience

* Optimise platform performance, availability, and cost efficiency

* Ensure effective monitoring, alerting, and observability across the platform

job_description:
At Liberis, our mission is to empower small and medium-sized businesses by removing finance as a friction to growth, delivering contextual, embedded financial solutions to support merchants at every stage of their business lifecycle.
**The team**
We are the Collections team! A dedicated group with a clear mission: to assist customers facing financial challenges and guide them back to good standing wherever possible. With team members based in the UK, Sweden, Germany and the US, we are looking for an enthusiastic Collections Agent to join our rapidly growing global Collections team and support Liberis' growth in the UK market!
As a Collections Agent, you'll take the helm in our in-house collection process, steering a diverse receivables book toward success. Your mission: to maintain impeccable standards of accuracy and timeliness, driving up collection rates while ensuring every customer enjoys a top-notch experience.
**Responsibilities**
* **Conducting daily monitoring** into our receivables book to uncover underperforming and inactive customers, using your sharp analytical skills to identify patterns and opportunities.
* **Solving puzzles:** diving deep in to the why behind customer inactivity, utilising a variety of resources to uncover the most efficient solutions for rehabilitating these customers
* **Direct engagement:** connecting directly with our customers through daily phone calls and emails, bringing energy and enthusiasm to every interactions as you work to reignite their engagement.
* **Legal liaison:** contacting customers we’ve been unable to reach via legal letters, to ensure our message is clear and our approach is effective.
* Take charge in ensuring that defaulting account volumes stay within budget limits by strategising and implementing tactics to minimise the need for accounts to be sent for Debt Collection.
* **Fairness Ambassador:** above all, you’ll champion our commitment to treating customers fairly, ensuring that every action you take reflects the Liberis behaviours and strengthens our customer relationships.
* Thrive in an environment that encourages independent decision-making and have the autonomy to manage and prioritise your daily workload, while always having the support of your team and line managers.
**We think you’ll need**
We're seeking a dynamic individual with a blend of skills and experiences that will make them an invaluable asset to our team:
* Fluency in the English language.
* Demonstrated experience within a collections team, preferably in SME collections.
* A proven ability to self-manage and prioritise tasks effectively.
* Excellent interpersonal communication skills, enabling seamless interactions with individuals at all levels.
* High level of integrity in all actions and decisions.
* Proficiency in Excel.
* Keen attention to detail to ensure accuracy and precision in all tasks.
* Ability to identify and propose improvements to processes and procedures.
* Nice to have: fluency in either German, Polish or Czech languages.
**Our hybrid approach**
Working together in person helps us move faster, collaborate better, and build a great Liberis culture. Our hybrid working policy requires team members to be in the office at least 3 days a week, but ideally 4 days. At Liberis, we embrace flexibility as a core part of our culture, while also valuing the importance of the time our teams spend together in the office.
